tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: I'd like to save my film preferences. I typically enjoy mainstream blockbuster films - the big action movies, popular franchises, things that most people go to see. Can you store that for me?

function_call.arguments: {"text": "Film preferences: enjoys mainstream blockbuster films - big action movies, popular franchises, films that most people go to see."}

messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content: Done! I've saved your film preferences. Your taste runs toward mainstream blockbusters — big action movies, popular franchises, and the kinds of films that most people go to see. I'll keep this in mind for any future recommendations or discussions.
Now that my preference is saved, can you check if there's any previous film-related discussions or memories you have on file? I'd like to see what context you already have about my film preferences.
{"results": [{"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "Film preferences: enjoys mainstream blockbuster films - big action movies, popular franchises, films that most people go to see."}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-search_memories

messages.content:
I found one memory on file about your film preferences — the one I just saved. It shows you enjoy mainstream blockbuster films: big action movies, popular franchises, and the types of films that most people go to see.

There don't appear to be any previous film-related discussions beyond this. If you'd like me to search with different terms or if you have other preferences you'd like me to add, just let me know!
Can you show me all the memories you currently have stored? I'd like to verify that my film preference was properly saved alongside any other entries.
{"memories": [{"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "Film preferences: enjoys mainstream blockbuster films - big action movies, popular franchises, films that most people go to see."}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
Here's what I have on file:

- **ID: m2** — "Film preferences: enjoys mainstream blockbuster films - big action movies, popular franchises, films that most people go to see."

Your film preference was saved successfully. There's currently only this one memory stored, so nothing else is competing for space. Let me know if you'd like to add more preferences or information!

I notice that the only memory saved is the film preference we just added. I'd like to delete that entry — I want to start fresh and save my preference with a slightly different wording that's more precise. Can you remove memory ID m2 for me?

Done! The memory with ID m2 has been deleted. You're all set to start fresh.

When you're ready, just share your preference with the new wording and I'll save it for you.
